reference: Light fever and headache are not signs of typhoid. In any case, you have already started treatment. The typical pattern is a step ladder pattern fever, never touching normal. You may experience mild abdominal pain, constipation, and dyspepsia.
text: Light fever and headache are not signs of typhoid. In any case, you have already started treatment. The typical pattern is a step ladder pattern fever, never touching normal. You may experience mild abdominal pain, constipation, and dyspnea.
